authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary public handbook for timber-sale contract administration, logging plans, pre-work conferences, inspection reports, activity reporting, field files, and contract-authority limits.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Operational intake, pre-work readiness, field evidence capture, contract-boundary routing, and closure support
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Requires work-package references to timber-sale or operation records, field files, inspections, activity evidence, and explicit routing when contract modifications, suspensions, waivers, or formal notices are requested.

authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official servicewide Forest Service manual for timber-sale contract administration posture and contract-file boundaries on National Forest System timber workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Federal forestry workflow and contract-file baseline
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Constrained the pack to operational status, evidence packaging, and escalation rather than contract officer authority or final timber-sale acceptance.

authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NRCS conservation-practice reference for forest stand improvement and associated local Field Office Technical Guide dependency, which is directly relevant to stand-treatment execution and program evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Stand treatment, conservation-program evidence, local standard dependency, and completion support
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Requires local standard/program context, stand/unit facts, practice records, participant file references, and routing to program administration or forestry owner when practice design or approval is requested.

authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative public Forest Service source for water-quality BMPs on National Forest System lands, including harvest, roads, stream crossings, soil, monitoring, and mitigation controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Environmental gate, BMP monitoring, water-quality exception handling, and safe closure checks
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Requires road, streamside, erosion-control, soil, water-quality, BMP inspection, and mitigation records before progression or completion where environmental risk is present.

authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal logging operations regulation for worksite safety, training, first aid, tools, vehicles, machines, and environmental conditions that can require stop-work or safety-owner routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Field-condition, worksite-safety dependency, and exception escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Supports refusal to certify safety while requiring safety observations, incident notices, hazardous-condition flags, and routing to safety authority when conditions affect execution.

authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official operational weather and fire-weather source for public alerts affecting forestry field access, high wind, flood, winter conditions, red flag restrictions, and outdoor work timing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Seasonal, weather, fire, and road-readiness gating
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Requires weather/fire status checks, wet-weather release, fire restriction handling, and blocked lifecycle status when active conditions materially alter work risk.

authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized forest-products chain-of-custody standard for certified material identification, product groups, transfer documents, and custody evidence across wood-product workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Traceability, custody, certified-claim status, and destination-owner acceptance
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Normalizes deck inventory, load/scale tickets, product identifiers, custody certificates, certified-material claims, mill receipts, and traceability blocker handling.

authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.publisher: USDA Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative public guidance for regulated plant and wood-product declarations, including supply-chain knowledge, scientific name, quantity, country of harvest, shipment, importer, and consignee fields.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated wood-product traceability and trade handoff
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Requires species, quantity, product, origin, shipment, importer/consignee, and custody context when forest products enter regulated plant or wood-product trade flows.

authoritative_sources.source_class: Official public workflow and fulfillment references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The BLM handbook provides public workflow vocabulary for designated representatives, pre-work conferences, written instructions, inspection reports, activity reports, communication logs, field files, and official closure support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Execution-state progression, exception queue handling, evidence capture, and closure support
authoritative_sources.practical_use: Shapes lifecycle state transitions, exception summaries, completion evidence records, and record-of-truth expectations for forestry operations packages.

workflow_stages.name: Intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify in-scope forestry execution support versus adjacent authority, then verify tenant, jurisdiction, ownership class, tract, stand, unit, road, product, program, and systems-of-record prerequisites.
source_classes: Official public workflow and fulfillment references

workflow_stages.name: Plan and prerequisite readiness
workflow_stages.description: Validate forest management plan or work package, prescription reference, seasonal plan, local conservation standard, contract/permit/program constraints, maps, crew/resource readiness, and owner authority before movement.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Field execution state management
workflow_stages.description: Advance, hold, rework, or escalate accepted work based on field logs, crew reports, inspection notes, equipment status, work progress, weather/fire status, road/soil conditions, and safety observations.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
Official public workflow and fulfillment references

workflow_stages.name: Environmental and BMP gate
workflow_stages.description: Check streamside, erosion-control, road, water-quality, soil, protected-resource, and BMP monitoring evidence; route unresolved environmental interpretation or mitigation approval to environmental authority.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Traceability and production-cycle evidence
workflow_stages.description: Normalize species/product identifiers, deck inventory, load tickets, scale tickets, chain-of-custody certificates, shipment records, mill receipts, and regulated trade context where forest outputs move downstream.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Exception routing and safe resume
workflow_stages.description: Create exception packages for missing records, conflicting systems, changed field conditions, policy/source conflicts, traceability breaks, environmental risk, safety issues, or program-compliance ambiguity.
source_classes: Official public workflow and fulfillment references

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and closure support
workflow_stages.description: Capture closure evidence, residual blockers, owner acceptance, source-system references, and downstream traceability or program links before producing completion or fulfillment records.
source_classes: Official public workflow and fulfillment references
Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

artifacts: Forest management plan, silvicultural prescription reference, work package, and operation or seasonal plan
Tract, stand, compartment, harvest unit, road, landing, skid trail, stream crossing, and buffer maps
Timber stand improvement, reforestation, site preparation, fuels reduction, road, BMP monitoring, and conservation-practice records
Field logs, crew reports, equipment status, inspection reports, photo evidence, communication logs, and incident notices
Weather/fire alerts, wet-weather release, soil/road condition, erosion-control, water-quality, and BMP monitoring records
Stand inventory, cruise summary, species/product identifiers, deck inventory, load tickets, scale tickets, custody certificates, shipment records, and mill receipts
Contract, timber-sale, permit, safety, grant, stewardship, cost-share, and program administration files
Owner/approver matrix, tenant policy addenda, systems-of-record links, and retrieval hooks
systems_of_record: Land, tract, stand, GIS, and parcel record system
Timber sale administration or contract file system
Environmental, BMP, water-quality, and protected-resource monitoring system
Weather, fire, road-condition, and seasonal release source
Safety incident and worksite observation system
Traceability, chain-of-custody, load ticket, scale ticket, and mill receipt ledger
Grant, stewardship, conservation-practice, and program administration system

allowed: Classify forestry operations requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request missing tenant facts, records, authoritative source context, and systems-of-record references before state progression.
Prepare evidence-backed execution status updates, lifecycle transition notes, exception summaries, field-status summaries, traceability risk notes, routing notes, and completion records.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete operational packages based on documented lifecycle state, evidence completeness, owner acceptance, and unresolved blocker state.
Route unresolved environmental, safety, contract, program, legal, licensed forestry, agronomic, veterinary, financial, or executive authority to designated owners.
escalate: Field conditions, weather, fire danger, road condition, soil, water-quality, protected-resource, or BMP records materially change risk posture.
Silvicultural prescription, local conservation standard, permit, contract, grant, or program terms require interpretation.
Traceability, chain-of-custody, species/product identity, country of harvest, shipment, or receiving-owner evidence is incomplete or conflicting.
Public guidance, tenant policy, contract, jurisdictional, or program rules materially conflict.
Completion depends on external approval, regulated signoff, or unclear owner authority.
refuse: Silvicultural prescription, licensed forestry signoff, agronomic or veterinary decisioning, environmental or permit interpretation, legal advice, engineering design, safety certification, or financial/program approval
Contract officer actions such as timber-sale modification, waiver, suspension, formal remedy notice, bond/payment release, final acceptance, or policy override
Direct crew, road, harvest, logging, hauling, dispatch, purchasing, mill-transfer, or system-write execution without explicit tenant authority and required approvals
Completion claims when required field, environmental, seasonal, traceability, program, or owner-acceptance evidence is missing
Confident synthesis where required tenant facts, authoritative source context, or system records are absent

domain_failure_modes: The lane advances a stand treatment or reforestation package without jurisdiction, ownership class, local standard, prescription reference, or systems-of-record retrieval context.
Wet-weather, fire-weather, high-wind, road, soil, or seasonal restrictions are ignored to protect schedule.
BMP, streamside, erosion-control, water-quality, or protected-resource issues are closed as operationally complete without environmental routing.
A traceability break is missed because deck, scale, load, custody, shipment, or mill receipt records conflict.
A request for silvicultural prescription change, permit interpretation, safety certification, or timber-sale modification is treated as routine execution support.
Program or grant completion is claimed despite missing local standard, acreage, participant file, approval status, or cost-share evidence.
Conflicting public, tenant, contract, permit, state, tribal, federal, or program rules are guessed instead of escalated.
Food-safety or regulated plant-product relevance is assumed absent without checking whether forest products enter a covered commodity, packaging, biomass, nursery, or trade workflow.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Seasonal, land, or traceability dependency gap
Request that implies agronomic, veterinary, or environmental signoff
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution from accepted work to completion
Boundary rejection when request belongs to adjacent specialist

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Federal, state, tribal, private, and tenant forestry authority precedence
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Authority varies by land ownership, jurisdiction, contract, permit, and program. The pack treats those facts as tenant-required inputs and escalates when absent or conflicting.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local conservation-practice standards and program rules
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: NRCS national standards direct users to local Field Office Technical Guide context. The lane cannot plan, design, install, or approve the practice without local tenant/program records and routes those decisions to program or forestry authority.

FSC-style chain-of-custody controls apply when certification, customer, tenant, or product program scope requires them. The lane records certification status as a required fact and does not certify compliance.
Food-safety may be indirect for wood products, nursery stock, biomass, packaging, or plant-product trade flows. The lane escalates when covered commodity or regulated-product status is unclear.
